title: The Impossible City
author: Karen Cheung
comment: Parts were fantastic and made me feel like I was living real life in Hong Kong. Some sections were too specialized in the author's arts scene, but the pressure of daily life and choices really made me think.
ai_comment: A raw, affectionate map of Hong Kong that balances grief, humor, and the weight of belonging.

title: The Book of Tokyo
author: M.Emnrich et al
ai_comment: Snapshots of Tokyo that feel like overheard conversations and fleeting neon reflections.

title: Strange Houses
author: Uketsu
comment: Easy-to-read crime novel that hooks you quickly and keeps the story moving. I liked it a lot.
ai_comment: Short, uncanny folktales where the walls are listening and the exits keep moving.

title: XI - A Study in Power
author: Kerry Brown
comment: A very detailed look at Xi's politics. Interesting and one more step in my journey to understand the China to come.
ai_comment: Coolly argued biography that reads like a blueprint for consolidated power.

title: Cold Enough for Snow
author: Jessica Au
ai_comment: A quiet river of a novella that asks how memory edits love and duty.

title: The Wind-Up Bird Chronicle
author: Haruki Murakami
ai_comment: Surreal wells, missing cats, and the sense that reality might be one hallway over.

title: Heaven
author: Mieko Kawakami
ai_comment: Bleak and tender; examines cruelty at school without easy absolution.

title: Butter
author: Asako Yuzuki
ai_comment: Food writing meets true crime; rich meals used to expose class and hunger.

title: Mild Vertigo
author: Mieko Kanai
ai_comment: Domestic repetition rendered hypnotic, as if watching thoughts loop in real time.

title: The Last Childeren of Tokyo
author: Yoko Tawada
ai_comment: Post-Fukushima dystopia drawn with delicate absurdity.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
ai_comment: High-school-teacher-in-space story powered by puzzles and dad jokes.

title: Piranesi
author: Susanna Clarke
ai_comment: Marble halls, tides, and a narrator who loves the labyrinth more than answers.

title: City on Fire: The Fight for Hong Kong
author: Antony Dapiran
ai_comment: Clear, reporterly walk-through of 2019 protests with legal context.

title: The Hole
author: Hiroko Oyamada
ai_comment: Languid surrealism about falling out of work and into a literal hole.

title: Cursed Bunny
author: Bora Chung
ai_comment: Body horror fables with a sardonic narrator; brings its own cursed energy.

title: Dream of Ding Village
author: Yan Lianke
ai_comment: Village-level tragedy of blood selling; mordant and musical.

title: Tokyo Ueno Station
author: Miri Yu
ai_comment: Ghostly monologue of a laborer haunting the park he once slept in.

title: Life Ceremony
author: Sayaka Murata
ai_comment: Inventive, grotesque rites that question norms with deadpan delivery.

title: Scattered All Over the World
author: Yoko Tawada
comment: I loved the idea and was fascinated by the unique writing style. The flow and writing stood above the story, but the story was still interesting enough to keep me engaged.
ai_comment: Playful linguistics in a world without Japan; language as migration map.

title: We Do Not Part
author: Han Kang
comment: This felt focused on inner emotions and relationships more than physical context. I like Han Kang, but sometimes I struggled to follow every thread and what it all meant.
ai_comment: Fragments of grief and art practice; soft but razor-edged.

title: Vanishing World
author: Sayaka Murata
comment: This book hangs on its core idea. It is far-fetched but still connected enough to today's society to keep me very interested, especially in how human relationships may evolve.
ai_comment: Breezy apocalypse vignettes that treat oddity as common sense.

title: In Praise of Shadows
author: Junichiro Tanizaki
comment: Fantastic. Some parts are outdated, but most ideas still hold. It made me think about not only light, but also what light takes away and how the unseen can be beautiful.
ai_comment: A meditation on darkness and lacquerware; still feels current about tech and taste.

title: Earthlings
author: Sayaka Murata
ai_comment: Deadpan alienation turning into something feral and unforgettable.

title: The Night of Baba Yaga
author: Akira Otani
ai_comment: Forest folklore retold with modern dread; slow-build eeriness.

title: Empire of AI
author: Karen Hao
ai_comment: Reported look at AI power centers; maps regulation gaps and incentives.

title: The White Book
author: Han Kang
comment: I hope I pick this up again. The format feels new and intriguing to me.
ai_comment: Lyrical meditation on loss using the color white as scaffolding.

title: Morgon og Kveld
author: Jon Fosse
ai_comment: A lyrical novella about a fisherman's birth and final day that quietly meditates on mortality faith and crossing into the unknown.

title: The Coming Wave
author: Mustafa Suleyman
ai_comment: Suleyman argues that AI and synthetic biology will arrive fast and asks whether institutions can contain their risks before they scale.

title: Abroad in Japan
author: Chris Broad
ai_comment: Chris Broad recounts his years in Japan through travel stories culture clashes and sharp humor from rural life to big city adventures.

title: Factfulness
author: Hans Rosling
ai_comment: Rosling uses global data to challenge common myths and shows how bias and fear distort how we judge progress in the world.

title: Pachinko
author: Min Jin Lee
ai_comment: A multigenerational saga of a Korean family in Japan tracing love survival and discrimination from colonial Korea into the modern era.

title: Klara and the Sun
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
ai_comment: An Artificial Friend observes human love class and illness while trying to save the girl she was bought to companion.

title: Weasels in the Attic
author: Hiroko Oyamada
ai_comment: Three linked surreal stories follow a couple and their circle as parenthood anxiety and social pressure turn ordinary life uncanny.

title: Strange Pictures
author: Uketsu
ai_comment: Interlocking mystery cases are solved through unsettling drawings whose hidden clues connect multiple crimes and buried motives.

title: The City and Its Uncertain Walls
author: Haruki Murakami
ai_comment: A man searches across reality and a shadowless walled city for a lost love in a dreamlike meditation on memory and identity.

title: The Three-Body Problem
author: Cixin Liu
ai_comment: Set against and after the Cultural Revolution this first-contact saga links frontier physics to a looming alien threat to humanity.

title: After Dark
author: Haruki Murakami
ai_comment: Over one Tokyo night strangers collide in bars hotels and backstreets as loneliness violence and compassion briefly intersect.

title: Norwegian Wood
author: Haruki Murakami
ai_comment: Toru Watanabe looks back on youth and grief as he is torn between fragile Naoko and vibrant Midori.

title: The Factory
author: Hiroko Oyamada
ai_comment: Three workers drift through a vast absurd factory where repetitive labor slowly erodes purpose identity and a stable sense of reality.

title: Your Utopia
author: Bora Chung
ai_comment: In darkly satirical speculative stories Bora Chung explores surveillance technology and intimacy while exposing the human cost of conformity.

title: The Gene
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
ai_comment: Mukherjee traces the science and history of genetics and examines the ethical stakes of heredity diagnosis and gene editing.

title: Homo Deus
author: Yuval Noah Harari
ai_comment: Harari explores how data capitalism biotechnology and AI could reshape human goals and create new moral and political risks.

title: The Hour of the Predator
author: Alex Scarrow
ai_comment: In this TimeRiders entry a botched mission strands Liam in the age of dinosaurs where survival threatens to rewrite history.

title: Pyongyang: A Journey in North Korea
author: Guy Delisle
ai_comment: Guy Delisle's graphic memoir records his tightly controlled work stay in Pyongyang with dry humor and sharp observations of authoritarian life.

title: Flashlight
author: Susan Choi
ai_comment: After a father disappears during a beach walk this family saga spans decades and countries to examine grief identity and political history.

title: Strange Buildings
author: Uketsu
ai_comment: The story follows eleven strange buildings that at first seem like separate mysteries. As the narrator studies their designs and histories the clues slowly connect and reveal one disturbing secret behind why the buildings were created.

title: The Monocle Companion - Better World
author: Monocle
ai_comment: Fifty ideas that can improve everyday life in cities and communities.
